title: How to Payment Gateways for Cameroon Digital Products: The Complete Creator Guide
author: Unchained Commerce
excerpt: Selling digital products from Nigeria, Ghana, Kenya, Pakistan, or Bangladesh is possible with crypto-native infrastructure that has no geographic restrictions.

content.text: In Cameroon, selling digital products online can be a daunting task due to payment restrictions. Many digital creators face the challenge of accessing international markets without being able to accept payments from popular platforms like PayPal or Stripe. You're not alone in this struggle. This guide is designed to walk you through a step-by-step process to create a payment gateway for your digital products in Cameroon, using Web3 technologies for global access.
Why This Method Works When Everything Else Fails

Blockchain-based payment solutions provide a decentralized and secure way to accept payments from any part of the world. With Web3 Wallet Connect, you can integrate a frontend wallet into your digital product store, allowing buyers to pay in their preferred cryptocurrency. This eliminates the need for users to create an account or go through lengthy verification processes, making it an attractive option for Cameroon-based creators who want to reach a global audience.
What You Need Before You Start

Before you begin, you'll need a few essential tools. First, create a digital product store using a platform like Unchained Commerce's Shop, which provides a simple and intuitive interface for creating and managing your store. Next, acquire the Web3 Wallet Connect plugin, which costs $19.99 and offers frontend wallet integration with multi-chain QR code generation and live price feeds. Finally, set up a payment processor like NOWPayments, which supports over 300 cryptocurrencies and doesn't require KYC verification.
Setting Up Your Payment and Delivery Pipeline

To set up your payment and delivery pipeline, follow these steps: Integrate the Web3 Wallet Connect plugin into your digital product store. Configure the payment processor to receive payments in the cryptocurrency of your choice. Set up automated delivery for your digital products, ensuring that buyers receive their purchases immediately after payment. Finally, configure your store's settings to reflect the new payment and delivery pipeline.
Don't let payment restrictions hold you back from reaching a global audience with your digital products.
Going Live and Making Your First Sale

Creators in Ethiopia, Cameroon, Tanzania, Venezuela, Iran, and the Philippines face a specific challenge: not only are conventional payment platforms unavailable or severely limited, but the local banking infrastructure may add additional friction to receiving international transfers even through alternative channels. Crypto payments remove this layer entirely. A seller in Addis Ababa and a seller in Amsterdam receive USDT transactions through the same infrastructure, at the same speed, with the same fees. The blockchain has no knowledge of which country a wallet is located in. This geographic neutrality is the core property that makes crypto-native infrastructure the right foundation for creators in restricted markets.
Scaling Once the System Is Running

The Full Stack Bundle brings together every component needed to build a global digital store: the Core Shop Engine for backend payment verification and automated delivery, Web3 Wallet Connect for the buyer-facing checkout experience, and the Admin Dashboard for transaction tracking and order management. For creators in restricted markets, this is not a luxury — it is the minimum viable infrastructure for a professional digital business. The bundle includes a deployment guide, priority updates, and Discord support. Total cost is a one-time payment of $79.99. There are no monthly fees, no percentage cuts on sales, and no platform relationship that can be terminated by someone else's compliance decision.
How can creators in Africa sell digital products internationally?

The most practical path for creators in restricted countries is a combination of NOWPayments for payment processing and USDT on Tron for the primary payment coin. USDT is a stable coin pegged to the US dollar, which means your revenue does not fluctuate with crypto market movements. Tron network fees are under one dollar, and settlement takes under 60 seconds. Once you receive USDT in your wallet, you convert to local currency through P2P exchanges — Binance P2P, Paxful, or local platforms specific to your country. The conversion typically takes 20-30 minutes and costs 2-5% of the transaction, which is significantly less than PayPal's international transfer fees or Western Union's rates. This is the complete payment flow that thousands of creators in Nigeria, Ghana, Kenya, Pakistan, and Bangladesh are using right now.
Web3 Wallet Connect provides a secure and decentralized way to accept payments from any part of the world.
Related: How Creators Like You Are Getting Paid

Creators in Nigeria, Ghana, Kenya, Pakistan, Bangladesh, and other restricted markets searching for international payment solutions are often looking for the same thing: a way to receive USD-equivalent payments from global buyers without a PayPal or Stripe account. Your Action Checklist

To create a payment gateway for your digital products in Cameroon, follow these concrete steps: Create a digital product store using Unchained Commerce's Shop, and purchase the Web3 Wallet Connect plugin. Set up a payment processor like NOWPayments, which supports over 300 cryptocurrencies. Integrate the Web3 Wallet Connect plugin into your store and configure automated delivery for your digital products. Promote your store to your target audience and monitor your sales data to optimize your sales strategy.
By using blockchain-based payment solutions, you can bypass traditional payment restrictions and increase your revenue.
